diagrams.title: Pointer Variable Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows how a pointer variable stores an address that points to another variable's memory location
elements: Variable x with value 10 stored at address 0x1000
Pointer variable ptr stored at address 0x2000
Arrow from ptr (value 0x1000) pointing to x
Memory boxes showing x=10 at 0x1000 and ptr=0x1000 at 0x2000
diagrams.purpose: Understand that pointers store addresses, not values

diagrams.title: Pointer Arithmetic on Different Types
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how pointer arithmetic scales based on data type size
elements: int pointer (4 bytes): adding 1 moves 4 bytes
double pointer (8 bytes): adding 1 moves 8 bytes
Memory row showing each type's jump distance

diagrams.title: Array Name as Pointer
diagrams.description: Shows array name acts as constant pointer to first element
elements: Array int arr[5] with elements at addresses 100, 104, 108, 112, 116
Pointer p can be set to arr and moved
